question: Looking at the US median income map, which statement below is the most accurate?

options.A: Georgia and Virginia likely have very similar median incomes.
options.B: All states shown have very diverse median income levels.
options.C: Iowa and New Jersey have similar median incomes.
options.D: The median income gap between Iowa and New Jersey might exceed that between Virginia and Georgia.

explanation: Iowa (83.9$k) and New Jersey (102.9$k) have a 19.0$k median income difference, while Virginia (54.7$k) and Georgia (55.5$k) have only a 0.8$k difference. However, the categorical color encoding groups Iowa and New Jersey into the same color bin (65$k+ range), making their large difference appear similar, while Virginia and Georgia appear more different due to different color bins.
